prompt_text:
### Demographics: 69-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 16 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes during urgent care evaluation for fatigue/blurred vision
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs; Intermittent GI discomfort (nausea/bloating)

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 3b (30-44)
Heart failure: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; Peripheral artery disease
Body mass index category: Normal
Osteoporosis (on bisphosphonate therapy)
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 3b (30-44); HF: HFmrEF (NYHA Class III); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Normal
- Family history: No family history of type 2 diabetes (elicited)
- Social history: Never smoker; Alcohol 5-6 drinks/week (1-2 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice daily; Semaglutide 1mg once weekly; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Insulin Degludec 20units once daily; Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily), Nitrofurantoin (100 mg twice daily for prophylaxis), Alendronate (70 mg weekly), Calcium Carbonate 500 Mg + Cholecalciferol 1000 Iu (daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 49.2 kg; Height 155.3 cm; BMI 20.3 kg/m²; Blood pressure 118/74 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 9.1%; 9 months ago 9.4%; 6 months ago 9.9%; 3 months ago 10.4%; current 10.5%), FPG (12 months ago 238mg/dL; 9 months ago 265mg/dL; 6 months ago 214mg/dL; 3 months ago 227mg/dL; current 229mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 49.7kg; 9 months ago 50.1kg; 6 months ago 47.7kg; 3 months ago 45.3kg; current 49.2kg), Height (12 months ago 155.3cm; 9 months ago 155.3cm; 6 months ago 155.3cm; 3 months ago 155.3cm; current 155.3cm), BMI (12 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 20.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 19.0kg/m²; current 20.3kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 120mmHg; 9 months ago 120mmHg; 6 months ago 120mmHg; 3 months ago 120mmHg; current 118mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 75mmHg; 9 months ago 76mmHg; 6 months ago 73mmHg; 3 months ago 74mmHg; current 74mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 14U/L; 9 months ago 7U/L; 6 months ago 4.9U/L; 3 months ago 6.1U/L; current 10U/L), AST (12 months ago 29U/L; 9 months ago 20U/L; 6 months ago 27U/L; 3 months ago 25U/L; current 25U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.3mg/dL; current 0.3mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 42mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 44mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 41mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 43mL/min/1.73m²; current 41mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 92mg/g; 9 months ago 103mg/g; 6 months ago 148mg/g; 3 months ago 109mg/g; current 157mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 70mg/dL; 9 months ago 76mg/dL; 6 months ago 70mg/dL; 3 months ago 71mg/dL; current 73mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 40mg/dL; 9 months ago 38mg/dL; 6 months ago 38mg/dL; 3 months ago 38mg/dL; current 39mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 133mg/dL; 9 months ago 117mg/dL; 6 months ago 136mg/dL; 3 months ago 137mg/dL; current 128mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 12.0g/dL; 9 months ago 11.2g/dL; 6 months ago 11.0g/dL; 3 months ago 11.0g/dL; current 10.7g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Negative (Current); fasting C-peptide 1.12 ng/mL (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
